name: Manhattan-Style Seafood Stew
recipeIngredient: 5 large Shallots or Green Onions, chopped
5 1/4 lb Canned Italian Plum Tomatoes, drained
1 lb White Potatoes, peeled, quartered lengthwise, and thinly sliced
1/2 lb Uncooked Medium Shrimp, peeled and deveined with tails intact
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a heavy large pot over medium heat until fat renders, about 5 minutes.
2. Add onions and shallots and sauté until tender, about 8 minutes.
3. Chop tomatoes in a food processor and add to the pot with clam juice, wine, bay leaves, and dried red pepper. Simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add potatoes to the stew and simmer until tender, about 20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
5. (Can be prepared 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Return to simmer before continuing.)
6. Add clams to the stew. Cover and simmer until clams begin to open, about 5 minutes.
7. Add scallops and shrimp, cover and simmer until clams open and scallops and shrimp are cooked through, about 3 minutes. Discard any clams that do not open.
8. Mix in half of the basil. Transfer stew to a large serving bowl. Sprinkle with remaining basil and lemon peel.
